_meta.phase: Phase 1 research draft; amended in Phase 2 to resolve audit gap (b)
_meta.amendment_note: Phase 2 audit (b) flagged that NR II best_practice (4,500 kg/ha) sat ABOVE the agronomic sanity band of 2,500-3,500 kg/ha because it encoded the modern-hybrid GENETIC CEILING rather than operational best practice. RESOLUTION: best_practice values were lowered to realistic operational best-practice (NR II = 3,300 kg/ha, within the sanity band), and the former hybrid-ceiling figures were preserved in a new field 'genetic_ceiling_kg_ha'. tobacco_math.py clamps projected yield to genetic_ceiling_kg_ha (the true achievable maximum) while model_design's base_potential (3,500, region-neutral) and these operational best_practice anchors keep point estimates realistic. No model behaviour regressed (NR II clamp remains 4,500).
_meta.confidence_note: National anchor points are well-sourced: documented Zimbabwe smallholder/communal averages cluster at ~1,900-2,050 kg/ha (Mount Darwin 2,052 kg/ha; Mazowe ~1,915 kg/ha), large-scale commercial historically ~2,500 kg/ha, and modern hybrid genetic ceiling ~4,500 kg/ha (Kutsaga). Per-Natural-Region splits across poor/average/best-practice are INTERPOLATED from these national anchors using the agro-ecological rainfall/reliability gradient and the regional multipliers in yield_model.py. Region IV/V cells remain the most uncertain (irrigation-dependent).

management_scenario_definitions.poor_management: Typical resource-constrained smallholder: late/erratic transplanting, sub-optimal basal + topdressing rates, weak weed/sucker control, rain-fed only, frequent late topping.
management_scenario_definitions.average_management: Typical commercial contract grower: full recommended fertiliser programme, scheduled spray program, supplementary irrigation in dry spells, timely topping and curing.
management_scenario_definitions.best_practice: Optimally managed, fully irrigated, fully resourced crop with strong genetics and tight curing control — realistic operational best practice (NOT the absolute genetic ceiling).
management_scenario_definitions.genetic_ceiling_kg_ha: Absolute achievable maximum for the region with modern hybrid genetics under ideal conditions — used only as the upper clamp in tobacco_math.project_yield.

I.description: Eastern Highlands and high-rainfall specialised farming zone (>1000 mm/yr; altitude ~1000-1700 m; cooler, longer wet season). Suited to diversified/plantation farming; tobacco grown on a limited scale, with cooler temperatures sometimes slowing ripening.
primary_constraints: Cooler temperatures can slow ripening/maturity
Higher disease pressure (angular leaf spot, frog-eye) under prolonged leaf wetness
Competition with higher-value horticulture/plantation crops for land
Steep terrain in parts limits mechanisation
sources: Vincent & Thomas (1960) Zimbabwe Natural Regions classification

II.description: Intensive farming region of the Mashonaland highveld (NR IIa/IIb; 750-1000 mm/yr; altitude ~1200-1500 m; reliable summer rains on light granite-derived sandy loams). The historic and current core of Zimbabwe's flue-cured tobacco belt (Mvurwi, Bindura, Marondera, Karoi, Mazowe).
primary_constraints: In-season dry spells (mid-season drought) in lower-rainfall IIb
Over-fertilisation / excess N degrading leaf quality
Bacterial (Granville) wilt hotspots (Mvurwi-Concession, Macheke/Headlands/Marondera)
Soil acidity on continuously cropped granite sands
sources: Masvongo et al. (2013) Mount Darwin smallholder study (2,052 kg/ha)

III.description: Semi-intensive farming region (650-800 mm/yr; mid-altitude; characterised by mid-season dry spells and higher temperatures). Substantial smallholder tobacco area — e.g. Mount Darwin / much of Mashonaland Central, which alone accounts for ~30% of national output.
primary_constraints: Mid-season dry spells requiring supplementary irrigation
Higher heat stress accelerating ripening and reducing leaf weight
Smallholder input-finance and curing-fuel (wood) constraints
Yield variability season-to-season tied to rainfall onset
sources: Masvongo et al. (2013) Mount Darwin (NR III): 2,052 kg/ha smallholder average

IV.description: Semi-extensive farming region (450-650 mm/yr; lower, hotter; periodic seasonal droughts). Marginal for rain-fed tobacco but increasingly planted with drought-tolerant varieties and, recently, with NCV (non-conventional/alternative) and irrigated tobacco initiatives in parts of Matabeleland.
primary_constraints: Insufficient and unreliable rainfall — irrigation effectively required for viable yields
High evaporative demand and heat stress
Limited tobacco extension/infrastructure outside core belt
Curing-fuel access and grower experience

V.description: Extensive farming / lowveld region (<450 mm/yr; hot, semi-arid). Generally unsuitable for rain-fed tobacco; any production is fully irrigated and small in area. Drought-tolerant lines (e.g. T75) can be grown experimentally but the region is not a commercial tobacco zone.
primary_constraints: Rainfall far below crop requirement — full irrigation mandatory
Extreme heat stress shortening the leaf-filling window
Negligible tobacco infrastructure and contracting presence
High production cost relative to yield
